paper_title: An Integration of Numerical Modeling and Paleoenvironmental Analysis Reveals the Effects of Embankment Construction on Long‐Term Salt Marsh Accretion

methods.description: Optically Stimulated Luminescence (OSL) analysis performed on coarse quartz grains from sediment core HOM19-2 to date the sequence and quantify sediment accretion rates over time. This method relies on the principle that quartz luminescence signals are reset by sunlight and accumulate over time due to natural radiation.

steps.description: Sampling of the core was designed to characterize all visible changes in core stratigraphy; a total of 12 samples were extracted from the core (HOM19-2-1 to HOM19-2-12, see Figure 5 for down-core sampling profile).
steps.searchable_summary: Extract sediment samples from core at specified intervals/locations

steps.description: To isolate the coarse quartz grains for equivalent dose measurement, all samples were treated with a 10% (v/v) dilution of 37% HCl to dissolve carbonates
steps.searchable_summary: Dissolve carbonates in sediment samples using HCl

steps.description: and with a 10% (v/v) dilution of H 2 O 2 to remove organic content.
steps.searchable_summary: Remove organic content from sediment samples using H2O2

steps.description: Dry sieving was used to extract grains 90-150 μm in dimeter for samples HOM19-2-2 and HOM19-2-4 and 90-125 μm for the rest of the samples.
steps.searchable_summary: Sieve sediment grains to specific size fractions (90-150 μm or 90-125 μm)

steps.description: Density separation using sodium polytungstate was performed to isolate the fraction of grains between 2.62 and 2.70 g cm −3 and remove feldspar and heavy metals grains.
steps.searchable_summary: Separate quartz grains by density using sodium polytungstate

steps.description: The quartz grains were then etched using 40% hydrofluoric acid to remove the outer portion of the grains contaminated by alpha particles and to remove any remaining feldspar grains.
steps.searchable_summary: Etch quartz grains with hydrofluoric acid (40%)

steps.description: Another wash with a 10% dilution of HCl was then performed to remove soluble fluorides produced in the previous step.
steps.searchable_summary: Wash etched quartz grains with HCl (10%)

steps.description: Grains were mounted as 2 mm-diameter multiple-grain aliquots on to 9.8 mm-diameter aluminum discs and used for analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: Mount quartz grains as multiple-grain aliquots on aluminum discs

steps.description: The luminescence measurements were made using an automated Risø TL/OSL DA-15 reader equipped with a 90 Sr/ 90 Y source (Bøtter-Jensen et al., 2003)
steps.searchable_summary: Measure luminescence signal using an automated OSL reader

steps.description: the quartz grains were stimulated with a light source consisting of blue (470 nm) light emitting diodes
steps.searchable_summary: Stimulate quartz grains with blue light (470 nm)

steps.description: and the luminescence signals were detected using a 5 mm thick U-340 filter and convex quartz lens beneath a photo-multiplier tube.
steps.searchable_summary: Detect luminescence signal using filter and photo-multiplier tube (PMT)

steps.description: The signal was recorded at 125°C for a total of 40 s
steps.searchable_summary: Record luminescence signal over time at specified temperature

steps.description: where the OSL signal was summed for the first 0.6-1.5 s and the background was calculated from 33.6 to 38.5 s of the decay curve.
steps.searchable_summary: Process OSL decay curve to sum signal and calculate background

steps.description: A 1.5% instrument measurement error was incorporated into the curve fitting.
steps.searchable_summary: Incorporate instrument measurement error into data processing

steps.description: Individual aliquots were accepted or rejected from D e calculation according to their performance during the following screening criteria (considering the associated uncertainties): (a) the response to the test dose was greater than three sigma above the background; (b) the test dose uncertainty was <20%; (c) the recycling ratios and (d) OSL-IR depletion ratios were within the range 0.8-1.2, and (e) recuperation was <5% of the response from the largest regenerative dose (7-8 Gy).
steps.searchable_summary: Apply screening criteria to accept or reject aliquots for equivalent dose calculation
inputs: Luminescence data (test dose response, recycling ratios, OSL-IR depletion ratios, recuperation)

steps.description: To date the Hesketh Out Marsh sequence and to quantify sediment accretion rates over time, OSL analysis was performed...
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate equivalent dose (De) for accepted aliquots and quantify sediment accretion rates

methods.description: Analysis of particle size distributions (PSDs) of sediment samples to infer sediment deposition processes (accretion vs. infilling) and perform end-member modeling analysis (EMMA).

steps.description: PSDs were measured for HOM19-1 at 10 mm intervals
steps.searchable_summary: Measure particle size distributions at 10 mm intervals along sediment core

steps.description: using a Coulter LS 13 320 Single-Wavelength Laser Diffraction Particle Size Analyzer that determines the dimensions of individual particles 0.375-2000 μm.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure particle size using a laser diffraction analyzer (Coulter LS 13 320)

steps.description: Subsamples were digested in 6% concentrated H 2 O 2 (50 ml of H 2 O 2 per 10 ml of sample) to remove any organic component
steps.searchable_summary: Digest subsamples in H2O2 to remove organic content

steps.description: with the samples then dispersed and sonicated in Na 6 O 18 P 6
steps.searchable_summary: Disperse and sonicate samples in sodium hexametaphosphate (Na6O18P6)

steps.description: and analyzed under sonicating measurement conditions.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze dispersed samples using particle size analyzer under sonicating conditions

steps.description: The resulting PSDs are the average of three repeats after elimination of outliers.
steps.searchable_summary: Average multiple PSD measurements and eliminate outliers

steps.description: The Coulter LS 13 320 undergoes regular calibration checks using samples with known size distributions.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform regular calibration checks for the particle size analyzer

steps.description: End-member modeling analysis (EMMA) was conducted using the EMMAgeo R package to statistically derive the most recurrent modes that explain the variance in the PSDs down-core profile (Dietze et al., 2012).
steps.searchable_summary: Perform End-member modeling analysis (EMMA) on down-core PSD data using EMMAgeo R package

steps.description: Hence, the maximum number of end-members that the model could detect in the down-core PSD profile was set as seven.
steps.searchable_summary: Set the maximum number of end-members for EMMA

steps.description: Particle size categories with several zero values were combined to enable end-member analysis of the entire data set.
steps.searchable_summary: Combine particle size categories with zero values for EMMA input

steps.description: A robustness test was performed to check on each end-member ( Figure S2a in Supporting Infomation S1).
steps.searchable_summary: Perform a robustness test on each EMMA end-member

steps.description: The loading of the end-members explaining most of the variance was plotted to observe what depositional processes (i.e., infilling or accretion) could be correlated with the PSDs in the core.
steps.searchable_summary: Plot the loadings of the EMMA end-members

steps.description: The down-core profile of the end-members scores were then plotted to observe changes in distribution along the core and attribute whether possible changes in accretion over time detected by the OSL in HOM19-2 were attributable to infilling or gradual accretion.
steps.searchable_summary: Plot the down-core profile of the EMMA end-member scores

methods.description: Measurement of the geochemical composition of sediment samples using X-Ray Fluorescence (XRF) to provide paleoenvironmental reconstruction and identify stratigraphical changes through geochemical proxies.

steps.description: The X-Ray Fluorescence (XRF) measurements for all HOM19-1 samples were used to produce the profiles of the following geochemical proxies: Si/K (silicates normalized toward mineral matter as sand indicator), Ca/K (calcium normalized toward mineral matter as shell content indicator), Fe/Mn (redox indicator), Rb (silt/clay mineral indicator); LOI was added to the list of geochemical proxies as organic content indicator (Plater et al., 2007).
steps.searchable_summary: Measure geochemical composition of sediment samples using X-Ray Fluorescence (XRF)

steps.description: The X-Ray Fluorescence (XRF) measurements for all HOM19-1 samples were used to produce the profiles of the following geochemical proxies: Si/K (silicates normalized toward mineral matter as sand indicator), Ca/K (calcium normalized toward mineral matter as shell content indicator), Fe/Mn (redox indicator), Rb (silt/clay mineral indicator)
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate geochemical proxies (Si/K, Ca/K, Fe/Mn, Rb) from XRF data
outputs: Calculated geochemical proxy values (Si/K, Ca/K, Fe/Mn, Rb)

steps.description: LOI was added to the list of geochemical proxies as organic content indicator (Plater et al., 2007).
steps.searchable_summary: Measure Loss on Ignition (LOI) for organic content (implied separate measurement)

steps.description: The X-Ray Fluorescence (XRF) measurements for all HOM19-1 samples were used to produce the profiles of the following geochemical proxies: Si/K, Ca/K, Fe/Mn, Rb... LOI was added to the list of geochemical proxies...
steps.searchable_summary: Plot down-core profiles of geochemical proxies (Si/K, Ca/K, Fe/Mn, Rb, LOI)

methods.description: Analysis of sediment composition using Near-Infrared (NIR) spectroscopy by measuring diffuse reflectance spectra and performing multiple regression analysis with known end-members.

steps.description: NIR spectra were measured by diffuse reflectance for all HOM19-1 samples using an integrating sphere on a Bruker MPA Fourier-Transform NIRS
steps.searchable_summary: Measure Near-Infrared (NIR) spectra by diffuse reflectance using FT-NIRS

steps.description: with NIR spectra based on combining 24 scans collected at 8 cm −1 intervals across the range 3,595-12,500 cm −1 (Russell et al., 2019).
steps.searchable_summary: Combine multiple NIR scans per sample
inputs: Raw NIR scans (24 scans per sample)

steps.description: NIR spectra were converted to first derivatives, calculated using a centrally weighted Savitzky-Golay smoothing algorithm minimizing spectral noise (Russell et al., 2019).
steps.searchable_summary: Convert NIR spectra to first derivatives using Savitzky-Golay smoothing

steps.description: The NIR spectra were interrogated using multiple regression of known end-member spectra onto the spectra derived for the unknown sediment samples (Russell et al., 2019).
steps.searchable_summary: Perform multiple regression of known end-member spectra onto sample spectra

steps.description: The multiple regression analysis was undertaken in R using the (LM) function in R (R Core Team, 2013)
steps.searchable_summary: Use the LM function in R for multiple regression analysis

steps.description: using mineral matter (powdered quartz SiO 2 ), CaCO 3 shell material and organic matter (International Humic Substances Society (IHSS) humic/ fulvic acid standards) as end-members
steps.searchable_summary: Use specified end-member spectra (quartz, CaCO3, organic matter) for multiple regression
inputs: Reference spectra for organic matter (IHSS standards)

steps.description: the output concentrations for these components are reported as weight percent ( Figure S3 in Supporting Infomation S1).
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate and report component concentrations as weight percent from regression results
